purpose: Quick validation: can new-brain TattleTots compete with the strongest baseline in each domain?
total_runs: 18 BMA runs (6 per domain × 3 seeds) + baselines already collected

coral_key.description: Target: beat A2 (AIS+SAR+Catch) at 62.7% detection (high adversary) and A3 at 37.6% (medium)

fire_ecology.description: Target: beat A2 Centralized at 247 burned (10 drones) and A1 Camera ML at 323 burned

grain_guard.description: Target: beat A0 Human IPM at 3.6% yield (orchard) and A3 Centralized at 4.9% yield (monoculture)

success_criteria.coral_key: BMA detection rate > A3 Full Centralized detection rate at same adversary level
success_criteria.fire: BMA burned cells < A2 Centralized burned cells at same drone count
success_criteria.grain: BMA final yield > best baseline yield at same landscape type

failure_interpretation.all_fail: New brains don't help — genome expressiveness isn't the bottleneck
failure_interpretation.coral_key_wins_others_lose: BMA advantage is domain-specific to adversarial residual detection
failure_interpretation.fire_wins_others_lose: BMA advantage is in physical coordination, not information processing
failure_interpretation.grain_wins_others_lose: BMA advantage is in heterogeneous landscapes, not time-series detection
failure_interpretation.all_win: Architecture is broadly competitive — proceed to full DOE with new brains
